bead.title: fix(agent): TestOSExecutor_EarlyCancel should cancel in <=10s not 60s
bead.description:
PROBLEM
  TestOSExecutor_EarlyCancel at cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go:1012
  spawns `sleep 60` with a 10-second context timeout and expects the
  early-cancel path to fire when the context expires. In the most recent
  audit run the test ran for 60.0 seconds, not the expected ~10 seconds.
  Either (a) the context cancel is not propagating to the subprocess kill
  signal, (b) the kill is firing but process-group cleanup waits for the
  full sleep, or (c) the early-cancel branch is wired up correctly but a
  separate wait loop is blocking the test goroutine until natural exit.
  Whichever the root cause, this is a real correctness gap: production
  agent execution relies on context cancellation killing runaway
  subprocesses promptly.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go:1012 TestOSExecutor_EarlyCancel
    invokes OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir with a 10s ctx timeout against
    `sleep 60`.
  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go:1019-1023 asserts
    result.EarlyCancel == true and result.ExitCode == -1.
  - The test PASSES (assertions are met) but takes ~60s wall-clock,
    suggesting one of:
      a. OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir's kill path is not signalling SIGKILL
         to the subprocess on context cancel.
      b. The kill signal fires but cmd.Wait() blocks until the OS reaps
         the process tree (no setsid, no -PGID kill).
      c. The function returns at 10s but the test goroutine is held by a
         separate mechanism (e.g. captured stdout reader not closing).
  - Read OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir at cli/internal/agent/agent.go (or
    wherever ExecuteInDir is defined) to determine which.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Investigate OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir; identify which of (a), (b), (c)
    explains the 60s wall-clock.
  - For each plausible cause:
      a. Add explicit kill on context cancel: when ctx.Done() fires before
         cmd.Wait() returns, send syscall.Kill(-cmd.Process.Pid, SIGKILL)
         (requires cmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}
         on Unix).
      b. Use process-group kill so descendants of the spawned subprocess
         also die.
      c. Close stdout/stderr pipes when ctx fires so any reader goroutines
         unblock.
  - Add explicit assertion that the test completes in under 15s wall
    clock: wrap test body with start := time.Now(); … ; require.Less(
    time.Since(start), 15*time.Second, "TestOSExecutor_EarlyCancel must
    not run longer than 15s").
  - Verify on Linux and macOS (cli is multi-platform per Makefile).

NON-SCOPE
  - General refactor of OSExecutor; only the early-cancel path.
  - Replacing OSExecutor with a different executor implementation.
  - Adding testing.Short() skip — the test SHOULD complete fast; if it
    doesn't, that's a bug. The fix is to make it fast, not to skip it.

INTERSECTIONS
  - No deps.
bead.acceptance:
1. The root cause of the 60s wall clock is identified and documented in the bead's commit message (one of: kill path not firing, process-group cleanup, reader-goroutine block, or other).
2. The fix is applied to cli/internal/agent/agent.go (or wherever OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir lives) so the test completes in well under 15 seconds wall clock.
3. TestOSExecutor_EarlyCancel in cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go:1012 contains an assertion that the test body completes in under 15 seconds (require.Less on time.Since(start)).
4. cd cli && go test -count=1 -run TestOSExecutor_EarlyCancel ./internal/agent/... completes in under 15s wall clock (verified by an external `time` measurement or test elapsed-time output).
5. result.EarlyCancel == true and result.ExitCode == -1 still hold.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
